Study Design & Problem Formulation: Formulate the multi-horizon forecasting objectives as distinct mathematical modeling tasks. Define the specific target variables for patient volume and operational resource pressure across short, medium, and long-term horizons.
Data Ingestion & Integration: Dataset is downloaded in data/raw. Structure the timestamped arrival and demographic records for sequential processing.
Data Cleaning & Quality Assurance: Systematically identify and impute missing clinical records and anomalous temporal gaps. Ensure absolute chronological integrity to prevent downstream computational errors.
Exploratory Time-Series Analysis: Apply rigorous statistical tests to assess stationarity and decompose the signal into underlying trend, seasonal, and residual components. Analyze auto-correlation to mathematically understand the decay of temporal dependencies.
Feature Engineering & Temporal Embedding: Construct predictive features such as lag variables, rolling statistical moments, and categorical time embeddings. Engineer specific covariates to capture influenza seasonality and day-of-week clinical patterns.
Data Splitting & Cross-Validation Strate: Implement a strict chronological data partitioning scheme to preserve temporal order. Utilize time-series cross-validation to rigorously validate model generalization and prevent data leakage.
Baseline Model Establishment: Compute naive forecasting benchmarks and simple moving averages to establish a foundational performance floor. This provides a baseline mathematical standard against which advanced models will be evaluated.
Short-Term Volatility Modeling: Train LightGBM and LSTM architectures to capture high-frequency variance and irregular arrival spikes over the 1 to 72-hour horizon. Optimize hyperparameters to map the non-linear dynamics of daily arrival and admission patterns.
Model Evaluation & Statistical Validatio: Quantify predictive performance using domain-specific loss functions including RMSE, MAE, and MAPE. Conduct residual analysis to ensure the models exhibit unbiased and statistically sound error distributions.
Business Impact Simulation: Translate the mathematical error metrics into actionable clinical outcomes by simulating resource allocation scenarios. Demonstrate the theoretical reduction in peak wait times and overflow staffing utilization.
Medium & Long-Term Trend Modeling: Fit structural time-series models, specifically SARIMAX and Prophet, to predict seasonal surges and multi-year demographic trends. Focus on isolating broader systematic signals over the 1 to 12-month horizon.
